document.text: 1st Battalion, Hampshire Regiment, was assigned to 11th Brigade throughout the First World War.
2nd Battalion, Hampshire Regiment, was assigned to 88th Brigade throughout the First World War.
2/5th Battalion, Hampshire Regiment, was assigned to 232nd Brigade from April 1917 to 31 March 1918.
The 2/5th Battalion was assigned to 233rd Brigade on 31 March 1918.
14th Battalion, Hampshire Regiment, was assigned to 116th Brigade from October 1915 to 23 February 1918, when the battalion disbanded.
15th Battalion, Hampshire Regiment, was assigned to 122nd Brigade from October 1915 until 1917.
15th Battalion, was redesignated 15th (Hampshire Carabiniers) Battalion on 8 October 1917.
11th Brigade was part of 4th Division throughout the First World War.
88th Brigade was part of 29th Division throughout the First World War.
122nd Brigade was part of 41st Division during the First World War.
116th Brigade was part of 39th Division during the First World War.
232nd Brigade was part of 75th Division during the First World War.
186th Brigade was part of 62nd Division in June 1914.
"A" and "B" companies of The 2/4th Battalion were part of Number 20 Beach Group in June 1943. "C" and "D" companies of The 2/4th Battalion were part of Number 21 Beach Group in June 1943.
Number 20 and 21 Beach groups landed on Sicily with the 51st (Highland) Infantry Division on 10 July 1943.
2/4th Battalion was part of 28th Brigade in February 1944.
28th Brigade was part of 4th Division in February 1944.
41st Division was part of X Corps in 1917. 41st Division was part of XVII Corps in 1917.
46th Division was part of X Corps in 1943.
75th Division was raised in the field by the Egyptian Expeditionary Force in 1917, and existed until 1920.
X Corps was part of the Second Army in 1917.
XVII Corps was part of the Third Army in March 1917.
The 2nd, 1/4th and 5th battalions were part of 128th Brigade in March 1944.
50th Infantry Division was part of XXX Corps during the Second World War.
XXX Corps was part of the Second Army during the Second World War.
1st Battalion, Hampshire Regiment, was part of the 231st Brigade during the Second World War.
231st Brigade was part of the 50th Infantry Division during the Second World War.
7th Battalion, Hampshire Regiment, was part of the 130th Infantry Brigade during the Second World War.
130th Infantry Brigade was part of the 43rd (Wessex) Division during the Second World War.
43rd (Wessex) Division was part of the XII Corps during the Second World War.
XII Corps was part of the Second Army during the Second World War.
21st Army Group was established in June 1943 and dissolved in August 1945.
21st Army Group was renamed the British Army of the Rhine (BAOR) on 25 August 1945.
Second Army was part of the 21st Army Group between June 1943 and August 1945.
The 10th Battalion was part of the 29th Infantry Brigade from June 1915 to 2 October 1916.
The 10th Battalion was attached to the 30th Infantry Brigade from 2 October 1916 to 7 October 1916.
The 10th Battalion was part of the 29th Infantry Brigade from 7 October 1916 to 2 November 1916.
10th Battalion was part of the 82nd Infantry Brigade from 2 November 1916 to 7 January 1919.
The 10th Battalion was part of the 66th Infantry Brigade from 7 January 1919 to 19 March 1919.
10th Battalion was part of the 85th Brigade from 19 March 1919 until disbanding on 31 December 1919.
The 82nd Infantry Brigade was part of the 27th Division during the First World War.
29th Brigade was part of the 10th Division during the First World War.
82nd Infantry Brigade was part of the 27th Division during the First World War.
27th Division was part of XVI Corps during the First World War.
The 12th (Service) Battalion formed in September 1914.
The 12th Battalion was part of the 79th Brigade from October 1914 to 19 April 1919.
The 12th Battalion joined a composite brigade under the commander of 77th Brigade on 19 April 1919.
Together with the rest of the composite 77th Brigade, the 12th Battalion joined 161st Brigade on 29 May 1919.
The 12th Battalion disbanded on 19 September 1919.
The 1/9th Battalion disbanded on 5 December 1919.
The 1/6th Battalion disbanded on 11 September 1919.
The 6th Battalion formed again in 1920.
The 79th Brigade was formed on 13 September 1914 as part of the Third New Army.
The 79th Brigade disbanded on 10 May 1919.
The 77th, 78th and 79th brigades were part of the 26th Division from 13 September 1914 to 10 May 1919.
The 85th Brigade was part of the 28th Division during the First World War.
The 26th Division was part of XII Corps during the First World War.
The 27th Division was part of XVI Corps during the First World War.
XII and XVI Corps were part of the Salonika Army during the First World War.
The 2nd Battalion was part of 14th Brigade during the Second Boer War.
The 14th Brigade was part of 7th Division during the Second Boer War.
The 9th Battalion was converted to The 157th Regiment Armoured Corps on 5 December 1941.
The 10th (Service) Battalion formed in August 1914.
The 11th (Pioneers) Battalion formed on 14 September 1914 in Dublin.
The 11th (Pioneers) Battalion joined 16th (Irish) Division on 20 September 1914 as divisional pioneers.
The 11th Battalion was disbanded in June 1919.
The 9th and 10th Battalions, Hampshire Regiment, formed again on 4 July 1940.
The 10th Battalion was converted to the 147th Regiment Armoured Corps on 25 November 1941.
The 9th and 10th Battalions joined The 201st Independent Infantry Brigade on 4 October 1940.
The 2/1st Hampshire Brigade formed in October 1914.
The 2/1st Hampshire Brigade was part of 2nd Wessex Division in October 1914.
The 2/4th, 2/5th, 2/6th and 2/7th battalions were part of 2/1st Hampshire Brigade in October 1914.
The Wessex Division formed in 1908.
The 43rd (Wessex) Division formed again in 1920.
The Wessex Division was numbered 43rd (1st Wessex) Division in May 1915.
The Hampshire Brigade was part of Wessex Division in 1908.
The 1/7th Battalion was part of Hampshire Brigade in October 1914.
The 1/9th Battalion relinquished its cycles in November 1915 and became an infantry battalion.
The 2/5th Battalion was part of the Secunderabad Brigade from January 1915 to March 1917.
The 2/7th Battalion was part of the Secunderabad Brigade from January 1915 to March 1916.
The 2/7th Battalion was part of Jubbulpore Brigade from March 1916 to August 1917.
The 2/7th Battalion was attached to 38th Brigade in September 1918.
The 232nd Brigade formed on 14 April 1917.
The 232nd Brigade joined the 75th Division on 25 June 1917.
The 2/4th Battalion, Hampshire Regiment, first formed in September 1914.
The 2/4th Battalion was part of 2nd Quetta Brigade from January 1915 to April 1917.
The 2/4th Battalion was part of 233rd Brigade from 25 May 1917 to 2 May 1918.
233rd Brigade was part of 75th Division on 15 May 1917.
The 2/4th Battalion was part of 186th Brigade from 6 June 1918 to 23 February 1919.
The 2/4th Battalion transferred to the 3rd Southern Infantry Brigade in April 1919.
The 2/4th Battalion was disbanded in October 1919.
186th Brigade was part of 62nd Division on 5 June 1918.
The 5th Battalion divided to form the 1/5th Battalion and 2/5th Battalion in September 1914.
The 7th Battalion divided to form the 1/7th Battalion and 2/7th Battalion in September 1914.
The 9th (Cyclist) Battalion formed in August 1914.
The 9th Battalion divided to form the 1/9th Battalion and 2/9th Battalion on 26 September 1914.
The 8th Battalion divided to form the 1/8th Battalion and 2/8th Battalion on 5 December 1914.
The 3/9th Battalion formed in April 1915.
The 3/4th Battalion formed in March 1915.
The 3/4th Battalion was renamed the 4th (Reserve) Battalion on 8 April 1916.
The 4th (Reserve) Battalion absorbed the 2/8th Battalion on 1 September 1916.
The 3/5th Battalion was renamed the 5th (Reserve) Battalion on 8 April 1916.
The 5th (Reserve) Battalion absorbed the 2/6th, 7th Reserve and 3/9th battalions on 1 September 1916.
The 4th (Reserve) Battalion absorbed the 5th (Reserve) Battalion in June 1917.
The 3/7th Battalion formed in March 1915.
The Hampshire Brigade Battalion formed on 13 February 1915. It was later renamed the 84th Provisional Battalion.
The Hampshire Brigade Battalion was part of 221st Mixed Brigade from 11 March 1915 to 20 May 1915.
The 10th Provisional Brigade was formed in May 1915.
The 84th Provisional Battalion transferred to 10th Provisional Brigade on 20 May 1915.
The 17th Battalion formed on 1 January 1917 from the 84th Provisional Battalion of the TF.
The 2/5th Battalion, Hampshire Regiment was disbanded in Palestine on 13 August 1918.
The 6th Battalion became the 1/6th Battalion in September 1914.
The 2/6th Battalion, Hampshire Regiment was formed in September 1914.
The 2nd Wessex Division was formed in October 1914.
The 2nd Battalion was part of 1st Guards Brigade in February 1940.
The 2nd Battalion came under command of 11th Infantry Brigade on 28 November 1942.
The 2nd Battalion was part of 1st Guards Brigade in November 1942.
The 2nd Battalion was part of 1st Guards Brigade between 18 February 1943 and 22 February 1943.
The 2nd Battalion came under the command of 128 Brigade on 27 February 1943.
The 2nd Battalion moved from 128th Brigade to 38th Brigade on 4 April 1943.
The 2nd Battalion moved from 38th Brigade to 12th Brigade on 11 April 1943.
The 2nd Battalion came under the command of 10th Infantry Brigade on 16 April 1943.
The 2nd Battalion ceased to be under the command of 10th Infantry Brigade on 26 April 1943, moving to Divisional Reserve.
The 2nd Battalion moved from 4th Division to 1st Division on 29 April 1943, coming under the command of 2nd Infantry Brigade.
The 2nd Battalion comes under the command of 38th Brigade (78th Division) on 4 May 1943.
The 2nd Battalion joined 128th Infantry Brigade on 10 May 1943.
128th Brigade moved to 1st Division on 10 May 1943.
The 4th Battalion divided to form the 1/4th and 2/4th Battalions in July 1939.
The 5/7th Battalion divided to form the 5th and 7th Battalions in August 1939.
The 4th Battalion formed from the 1st Volunteer Battalion when the Territorial Force was created in 1908.
The 5th Battalion formed from the 2nd Volunteer Battalion when the Territorial Force was created in 1908.
The 6th Battalion formed from the 3rd Volunteer Battalion when the Territorial Force was created in 1908.
The 7th Battalion formed from the 4th Volunteer Battalion when the Territorial Force was created in 1908.
The 12th Indian Division formed in March 1915.
The 33rd Indian Brigade formed in March 1915.
The 33rd Indian Brigade was broken up on 7 December 1915.
The 33rd Indian Brigade was part of 12th Indian Division between March 1915 and 7 December 1915.
The 1/4th Battalion was part of 33rd Indian Brigade between March 1915 and 7 December 1915.
The Portsmouth Brigade formed in 1889.
The Portsmouth Brigade was renamed Hampshire Brigade in 1902.
The Hampshire Brigade was numbered as 128th (Hampshire) Brigade in May 1915.
The 128th (Hampshire) Brigade re-formed as 128th (Hampshire) Infantry Brigade in 1920.
The 128th (Hampshire) Infantry Brigade was redesignated 128th Infantry Brigade in 1937.
128th Brigade was part of the 43rd Division from the start of the Second World War until 15 August 1942.
128th Brigade was part of 46th Division from 15 August 1942 to the end of the Second World War.
The Hampshire Militia became the 3rd (Hampshire Militia) Battalion in July 1881.
The 3rd (Hampshire Militia) Battalion became the 3rd (Special Reserve) Battalion on 21 June 1908.
The 3rd (Special Reserve) Battalion was disembodied on 3 July 1919.
Surplus from the 3rd (Reserve) Battalion formed the 13th (Service) Battalion on 31 October 1914.
The 13th (Service) Battalion was converted to the 13th (Reserve) Battalion in April 1915.
The 13th (Reserve) Battalion was redesignated the 34th Training Reserve Battalion on 1 September 1916.
The 34th Training Reserve Battalion was disbanded on 21 January 1918.
The 16th (Reserve) Battalion was formed in September 1915.
The 16th (Reserve) Battalion converted into the 96th Training Reserve Battalion in September 1916.
The 26th Division was created on 13 September 1914 for the Third New Army.
The 26th Division was disbanded on 10 May 1919.
The 1st (Garrison) Battalion, Hampshire Regiment formed in Portland in April 1916.
The 18th (Home Service) Battalion formed in Alton in December 1915.
The 18th (Home Service) Battalion disbanded in December 1917.
The 1st (Garrison) Battalion was renumbered as the 18th (Garrison) Battalion in June 1918.
The 70th (Young Soldiers) Battalion formed in Southampton on 26 September 1940 from the two "Young Soldier" companies of the 8th Battalion.
The 70th (Young Soldiers) Battalion disbanded on 1 July 1943.
The 280th Graduated Battalion became 51st (Graduated) Battalion, Hampshire Regiment, on 24 September 1917.
The 281st Graduated Battalion became 52nd (Graduated) Battalion, Hampshire Regiment, on 24 September 1917.
The 51st and 52nd battalions were part of 201st (2/1st Middlesex) Brigade in September 1917.
The 201st (2/1st Middlesex) Brigade was part of 67th (2nd Home Counties) Division in September 1917.
The 67th (2nd Home Counties) Division disbanded in March 1919.
